figures.caption: Figure 9 | Uncurated VCQ L-XL samples (6/8). An 8×8 grid of 64 randomly selected distinct ImageNet classes, disjoint from the other gallery pages. Sampling uses LXL with CodebookSize-Aware CFG, cosine CFG cfg=3 and power=1.0.

Figure 10 | Uncurated VCQ L-XL samples (7/8). An 8×8 grid of 64 randomly selected distinct ImageNet classes, disjoint from the other gallery pages. Sampling uses LXL with CodebookSize-Aware CFG, cosine CFG cfg=3 and power=1.0.

Figure 5 | Uncurated VCQ L-XL samples (2/8). An 8×8 grid of 64 randomly selected distinct ImageNet classes, disjoint from the other gallery pages. Sampling uses LXL with CodebookSize-Aware CFG, cosine CFG cfg=3 and power=1.0.

Figure 6 | Uncurated VCQ L-XL samples (3/8). An 8×8 grid of 64 randomly selected distinct ImageNet classes, disjoint from the other gallery pages. Sampling uses LXL with CodebookSize-Aware CFG, cosine CFG cfg=3 and power=1.0.

Figure 3 | Uncurated class-conditional samples from VCQ L-XL. A 4×8 grid covering visually diverse ImageNet classes with no manual curation. Samples use the Large Tokenizer (𝐾max =11264) and AR-XL (684M) with CodebookSize-Aware CFG at the best EVAL_virtual_npz gFID configuration: cosine CFG with cfg=3 and power=1.0.

Figure 2 | Entropy cliff and codebook schedule. (a) Remaining entropy: Constant (𝐾 =16384) exhausts the entire ∼15.6-bit information budget within the first 2 positions (entropy cliff), while VCQ spreads the information consumption over ∼6 positions; the language model (LAMBADA, ∼21.5 bits) consumes information smoothly, illustrating the ideal allocation for natural sequences. For clarity, this plot uses a Power schedule with 𝐾min =16. (b) Codebook size schedule: Constant is fixed at 𝐾=16384; VCQ increases gradually from 𝐾min to 𝐾max.

Table 8 | Disentangling VCQ and CodebookSize-Aware CFG contributions. Same VCQ model, different inference strategies. gFID w/o CFG is independent of the inference strategy and reflects modeling quality.

Figure 7 | Uncurated VCQ L-XL samples (4/8). An 8×8 grid of 64 randomly selected distinct ImageNet classes, disjoint from the other gallery pages. Sampling uses LXL with CodebookSize-Aware CFG, cosine CFG cfg=3 and power=1.0.

Table 5 | Reconstruction and generation comparison (ImageNet 2562 class-conditional). Naming format “Tok-AR” (B=Base, L=Large, XL=XLarge). rFID/PSNR are tokenizer reconstruction properties and are identical across rows that share the same tokenizer. “Diff.”=diffusion, “M+D”=mask+diffusion, “Mask.”=masked transformer, “HAR”=hierarchical AR, “PAR”=parallel AR. SSemantic/VFM: semantic regularization, semantic alignment, or frozen/pre-trained vision foundation model; CClustering: periodic token/codebook clustering; DDiff./FM: diffusion or flow-matching generator, decoder, token-distribution model, or loss; TDropout: tail dropout.

Table 1 | 𝑡∗ under 𝐾=16384 (log2 𝐾 = 14 bits/pos). Top: empirical 𝑡∗ for datasets from small-scale to internet-scale; five orders of magnitude of data buy only one extra position. Bottom: data thresholds 𝑁𝑚 = 𝐾𝑚−1 required to push 𝑡∗ to a given value; beyond 𝑡∗ =3 the required scale far exceeds any existing dataset.
Table 7 | CFG and temperature configurations corresponding to the reported gFID and gFID w/o CFG values.

Figure 11 | Uncurated VCQ L-XL samples (8/8). An 8×8 grid of 64 randomly selected distinct ImageNet classes, disjoint from the other gallery pages. Sampling uses LXL with CodebookSize-Aware CFG, cosine CFG cfg=3 and power=1.0.

Figure 8 | Uncurated VCQ L-XL samples (5/8). An 8×8 grid of 64 randomly selected distinct ImageNet classes, disjoint from the other gallery pages. Sampling uses LXL with CodebookSize-Aware CFG, cosine CFG cfg=3 and power=1.0.
Table 6 | Semantic aggregation. 𝐾min=2 forces the tokenizer to compress discriminative information into the earliest positions.
Figure 4 | Uncurated VCQ L-XL samples (1/8). An 8×8 grid of 64 randomly selected distinct ImageNet classes. Sampling uses LXL with CodebookSize-Aware CFG, cosine CFG cfg=3 and power=1.0.
Table 2 | Schedule parameters. 𝐾 is the average codebook size, BPP the bitrate, and 𝑡∗ the entropy cliff position.
